sprint: Sprint 41 -- V0.27.17 B-104 Step 1 server-side analytics authority (US-348/US-349 redo via architecture shift) + side fixes
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #12 in the V0.27 chain. THIRD cycle of the V0.27.7 US-326/US-328 false-pass class -- shipped through to V0.27.16 despite Marcus's I-040 discipline (synthetic-seam-mock passes do NOT count; real-drive round-trip + DB read-back is the gate). Argus's 2026-05-21 drill of V0.27.16 (drive 20 = 3,808 realtime rows x 16 params, ~9 min real engine running, sequencer poweroff, auto-boot, 8-poll bench observation) confirmed: US-348 server drive_summary computed fields STILL NULL (same NULL pattern as drives 12-19 pre-fix); US-349 Pi drive_statistics STILL zero rows ever. Argus's root cause hypothesis: DriveDetector's drive-end signal does not fire when drive is terminated by sequencer poweroff (no engine-off OBD signal before stack tears down); recorder is wired for FUTURE drive-end events but never catches THIS drive's actual end; subsequent boots don't retroactively process unfinished prior-boot drives. Argus: 'cannot recommend a third redo of the same form.' CIO directive 2026-05-21: advance B-104 (just filed 30 min prior) Step 1 architectural shift INSTEAD of writer redo. Pi = telemetry emitter; server = analytics authority. Server computes drive_summary + drive_statistics from raw realtime_data (3,808 rows for drive 20 already on disk); no Pi-side writer trigger required; bug class structurally impossible. Pi keeps drive boundary EVENT logging (drive_start / drive_end timestamps, etc.) for Pi-health/diagnostics -- these are event records, not derived analytics. Pi-side drive_statistics table RETIRED entirely (CIO ratification 2026-05-21). Plus 3 side fixes bundled per CIO ratification: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix; deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap (Argus's morning find); I-040 structural close (deploy-context drive simulator). Branched from sprint/sprint40-bugfixes-V0.27.16 tip @ 78c7c2d per chain-end-merge rule. PM Rule 10 design-gate DoD applies: server analytics writer is load-bearing for data pipeline -> specs/architecture.md update required in-sprint.
createdBy: Marcus (PM) Session 41 2026-05-21 per CIO ratification of B-104 Step 1 advance as Sprint 41 fix path. AskUserQuestion answers 2026-05-21: scope=hybrid (option 3) with Pi keeping event-log records for diagnostics + server as authority for derived analytics; Pi-side drive_statistics table retire entirely; backfill drives 12-20 in Sprint 41; Atlas briefed before scope-lock; all 3 side fixes bundled. Per-story acceptance + verification + gate criteria pre-registered by Atlas at Ralph dispatch (mirrors Sprint 39/40 cadence).

testBaseline.note: Inherited from V0.27.11 / Sprint 37 baseline (commit 6184a7f). Carried through V0.27.12-15 + V0.27.16. Re-snapshot after Sprint 41 lands -- B-104 Step 1 will touch server-side analytics surface meaningfully.

sprintNotes: THIRD CYCLE of the V0.27.7 US-326/US-328 false-pass class. Marcus + Atlas + Argus aligned: a third naive writer redo is not the answer. CIO directive: advance B-104 Step 1 architectural shift as the structural fix.

B-104 architectural principle: Pi = telemetry emitter, server = analytics authority. Pi may compute in-drive aggregates locally (engine on = AC power, no battery cost) but does NOT transmit derived analytics. Pi DOES log drive boundary events (drive_start / drive_end timestamps) for Pi-health/diagnostics -- these are event records, not derived analytics. Server is sole writer of persisted analytics tables.

Pi-side drive_statistics table RETIRED entirely (CIO 2026-05-21). No more Pi-side stats writer wiring. drive_summary Pi-side computed-field writer retired; Pi keeps event-log fields (drive_start_timestamp, ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, data_source) for diagnostics.

Server recompute trigger: overnight batch + on-demand recompute. Idempotent: same raw data + same logic = same output. Backfill of drives 12-20 (NULL drive_summary fields + missing drive_statistics rows) runs the new server compute path as the empirical validation of the architecture.

B-100 (drive_summary writer broken / empty shells), US-326 (V0.27.7 server analytics NULL fix that false-passed), US-348 (V0.27.16 redo that false-passed), US-328 (V0.27.7 Pi-side writer schema-only Option C), US-349 (V0.27.16 redo that false-passed) all SUPERSEDED on Sprint 41 land.

I-040 structural close (US-355): deploy-context drive simulator. The 3rd-cycle bug class shipped because unit-test fixtures don't reproduce deploy-time runtime conditions. The structural fix is a test surface that drives the integrated orchestrator + DriveDetector + recorder + sync + server compute path against a real database, NOT a mock of any seam. Marcus + Atlas + Argus + Ralph design at PRD-grooming time.

Side fix US-353: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix. Per Argus's drill: first post-deploy reboot tripped maxTrailBytes=65536 because trail accumulated unbounded while F-8 was broken. F-8 design SOUND post-fix; guard interaction non-chain-blocking but worth fixing alongside the deploy-hygiene + B-104 work.

Side fix US-354: deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap. Argus's morning find: V0.27.16 deploy wrote files to disk + bumped .deploy-version but did NOT restart eclipse-powerwatch.service + did NOT run systemctl daemon-reload. Pi was running V0.27.15 code in memory despite .deploy-version reporting V0.27.16/5837239. Argus worked around with daemon-reload + reboot on bench before drill. Would have produced false-negative F-7/F-8 validation if not caught.

Per-task acceptance/gate criteria pre-registered by Atlas at Ralph dispatch time. PM writes the sprint contract (intent + scope + groundingRefs + invariants); Atlas writes the acceptance + verification + per-task gates. Sprint 39/40 cadence preserved.

Tester /sprint-validated for Sprint 40 NOT RUNNING per Argus 2026-05-21: US-348 + US-349 fail acceptance; chain merge HELD per Mike 2026-05-08/10 chain-end-merge rule. regression_manifest F-008/F-011/F-012 stays HELD. Sprint 41 IRL re-validation is the unblock trigger.

US-346 Atlas T3 §10.6 design-gate sign-off STILL PENDING from Sprint 40. Argus's drill report noted: 'haven't seen Atlas T3-PASS sign-off; need confirmation before /sprint-validated.' Independent of Sprint 41 work but blocks /sprint-validated decision.

Argus's 2026-05-21 sprint.json standards audit (PASS w/ 4 flags) landed mid-spin. Flags 1+2 (validatesFeatures correction F-008/F-011/F-012 -> F-005+F-007; PM Rule 10 architecture.md gap closed via new US-356) + flags 3+4 (US-354 eclipse-obd verification + US-355 empirical anchor tightening to require RED on V0.27.7/V0.27.16 false-pass conditions) landed PM-side. 3 atlas-at-gate refinements routed to Atlas brief: US-351 'sensible values' quantitative spec; US-352 sparse-drive handling; US-353 multi-reboot scope. Argus audit file: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-21-from-argus-sprint41-sprint-json-standards-audit.md.

stories.title: B-104 Step 1a: server-side drive_summary compute from raw (US-326+US-348 redo)
stories.intent: B-104 architectural shift Step 1a. Server reads raw realtime_data + Pi-synced event logs (drive_start_timestamp + ambient_temp_at_start_c + starting_battery_v + data_source) + computes drive_summary fields (start_time, end_time, duration_seconds, row_count, is_real, analytics) on a server-side trigger that does NOT depend on a Pi-side drive-end signal. Pi-side drive_summary computed-field writer retired; Pi keeps event-log fields for diagnostics per Spool data v2 contract. Argus's root cause (DriveDetector drive-end signal does not fire on sequencer-driven termination) becomes structurally moot -- server reads timestamps from realtime_data MIN/MAX, doesn't depend on a marker event. Trigger options for Atlas at dispatch: (a) on every sync batch receipt for any drive_id touched, (b) on detection of a drive's last realtime_data row being older than N minutes (soak window), (c) overnight batch + on-demand. CIO ratified overnight batch + on-demand. Diagnostic-first: pre-flight rg sweep of existing US-326/US-348 server writer surface; document what is being retired vs. preserved.

groundingRefs.value: Argus drill 2026-05-21: drive 20 = 3,808 realtime_data rows x 16 parameter_names already on server post-sync; drive_summary row 27 has start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real all NULL. Same NULL pattern across drives 12-19. Server-side compute path CAN compute the right values from realtime_data MIN/MAX + COUNT -- the raw data is canonical and present.

Argus root cause hypothesis: DriveDetector's drive-end signal does not fire when drive is terminated by sequencer poweroff (no engine-off OBD signal before stack tears down). Server compute path that reads raw realtime_data + timestamps does NOT depend on a Pi-side drive-end marker -- structural fix.

B-104 architectural principle (CIO 2026-05-21): Pi = emitter, server = analytics authority. Pi keeps event-log records (drive_start / drive_end timestamps) for diagnostics; server computes derived analytics from raw event stream. Idempotent overnight batch + on-demand recompute.

invariants: Pi-side drive boundary event log (drive_start / drive_end timestamps) preserved for diagnostics (CIO 2026-05-21 ratified)
drive_summary server table schema unchanged (schema is fine; writer is the shift)
Pi-side sync client unchanged (raw data + event-log fields still flow; no new transmission)
Other server-side writers unchanged (US-351 owns drive_statistics)
B-104 Step 2+ scope deferred (GEM family, Mahalanobis -> V0.28+)

stories.title: B-104 Step 1b: server drive_statistics compute + retire Pi table (US-328+US-349 redo)
stories.pmSignOff: Marcus (PM) 2026-05-21 -- size L approved: 8 filesToTouch spans Pi-side retirement (4 files: drive_statistics.py DELETE, database_schema.py UPDATE, drive/detector.py UPDATE, orchestrator/lifecycle.py UPDATE) + Pi-side test retirement (2 files: test_drive_statistics_writer.py DELETE, test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py UPDATE-OR-DELETE) + new server-side surface (2 files: db/models.py UPDATE, analytics/drive_statistics_compute.py NEW). Retirement-plus-build symmetry is genuinely L-sized. CIO 2026-05-21 ratified Pi-side complete retirement; B-104 Step 1b lands the architectural shift; cannot meaningfully sub-divide without leaving the system in a half-migrated state mid-sprint.
stories.intent: B-104 architectural shift Step 1b. Server reads raw realtime_data + computes per-parameter_name stats (min / max / avg / std_dev / outlier_min / outlier_max) + writes to server-side drive_statistics table. Pi-side drive_statistics table RETIRED entirely (CIO 2026-05-21 ratified) -- migration drops it; DriveStatisticsRecorder removed from src/pi/obdii/drive_statistics.py and orchestrator wiring; tests retired. The V0.27.16 US-349 wiring (DriveDetector._endDrive -> recorder seam) goes away. The Pi-side schema declaration in database_schema.py + ALL_SCHEMAS registration goes away. Subsequent boots will not migrate the table; existing Pi rows (zero, per Argus) are not synced anywhere. Server-side trigger: overnight batch + on-demand (same path as US-350). Diagnostic-first: pre-flight rg sweep of all Pi-side drive_statistics surface to confirm complete retirement.

stories.title: Side fix: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix
stories.intent: Per Argus's drill 2026-05-21: first post-deploy reboot tripped maxTrailBytes=65536 guard on boot-progress trail. Trail accumulated unbounded while F-8 was broken (every shutdown classified crashed_during_operation -> trail never trimmed at CLEAN_COMPLETE). F-8 design SOUND post-US-345 fix; once trail trims on clean shutdowns the unbounded growth stops. Guard interaction is the issue: a one-time post-deploy trail-size cleanup OR a more permissive maxTrailBytes value OR a guard that auto-trims rather than refusing-to-write. Atlas + Ralph finalize the fix shape at dispatch. Argus's filed finding at tester/findings/2026-05-21-us-345-f8-fix-blocked-by-maxtrailbytes-guard.md has the technical detail (Argus-lane file; PM does not read directly per lane discipline; Atlas mediates if cross-lane reference needed). Non-chain-blocking; rides V0.27.17 alongside the B-104 work.

stories.title: Side fix: deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap
stories.intent: Per Argus's morning find 2026-05-21: V0.27.16 deploy wrote files to disk + bumped .deploy-version but did NOT restart eclipse-powerwatch.service + did NOT systemctl daemon-reload. Pi was running V0.27.15 code in memory despite .deploy-version reporting V0.27.16/5837239. Argus worked around with bench daemon-reload + reboot before drill. Would have produced false-negative F-7/F-8 validation if not caught (Test 2 would have re-reproduced the F-7 bug; F-8 CLEAN_COMPLETE evidence depends on the new unit being in systemd's in-memory transaction graph at shutdown time). Argus's recommended fix: weld systemctl daemon-reload + restart eclipse-powerwatch.service + eclipse-obd.service into deploy-pi.sh post-copy + pre-.deploy-version-bump + verify both come back active with new PID-start-time later than deploy start. Non-chain-blocking once worked around but every Pi deploy ships dead code until manual reboot.

tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi.py (UPDATE -- static check that the new daemon-reload + service-restart + PID-verification step is present in deploy-pi.sh)

Systemd unit file contents (US-345 etc. own those)
.deploy-version semantics (bump logic preserved; just relocate the bump to after the restart-verification)

stories.title: I-040 structural close: deploy-context drive simulator test surface
stories.intent: Structural close on the V0.27.7-V0.27.16 false-pass class (Tester I-040). Argus's analysis 2026-05-21: 'I cannot recommend a third redo of the same form.' Root cause of the recurrence: unit-test fixtures don't faithfully reproduce deploy-time runtime conditions (orchestrator initialization order; DriveDetector's drive-end signal vs. sequencer-driven termination; sync sweep cadence; trigger seam vs. real-data shape). The structural fix is a deploy-context test surface -- a 'drive simulator' that exercises the integrated orchestrator + DriveDetector + recorder + sync + server compute path against a real database (Pi-side SQLite + server-side MariaDB), driving from the same deploy artifact the IRL Pi runs. Marcus + Atlas + Argus + Ralph design at PRD-grooming time. This story may produce a test harness + a small number of seed scenarios; subsequent stories add scenario coverage. Non-chain-blocking but high-value for closing the discipline gap that allowed 3 cycles of the same bug to ship.
